channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Israel launches new airstrikes on Lebanon as leaders draw closer to ceasefire with Hezbollah
item.description: Israeli airstrikes are hitting Lebanon with airstrikes as negotiations progress toward a ceasefire between the country and Lebanon-based Hezbollah militants.
item.dc:creator: Sally Abou Aljoud, Joe Federman And Sam Metz, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Philippine showdown: President says he'll fight vice president's plot to have him killed
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. on Monday described a public threat by the vice president to have him killed by an assassin as a criminal plot and vowed to fight it, in a looming showdown between the country’s two top leaders.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Russia tricks Yemeni men to fight in Ukraine under Houthi scheme: report
item.description: Russia has begun luring Yemeni men to fight in its war in Ukraine by falsely promising lucrative jobs before forcing them to sign military contracts and then sending them to fight on the front lines.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Egyptian officials say 17 people are missing after a tourist yacht sank in high waves on Red Sea
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — At least 17 people are missing after a tourist yacht sank in the Red Sea following warnings about rough seas, Egyptian officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Russia reportedly captures a Briton fighting for Ukraine as Russian troops advance
item.description: Russia's military captured a British national fighting with Ukrainian troops who have occupied part of Russia's Kursk region, according to reports Monday, as Moscow began daylight drone attacks on civilian areas of Ukraine and its ground forces accelerated gains along parts of the front line.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The UAE arrests 3 Uzbek nationals for the killing of an Israeli-Moldovan rabbi
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — The United Arab Emirates said Monday police arrested three Uzbek nationals for the killing of an Israeli-Moldovan rabbi, an attack that's raised concerns for the burgeoning Israeli community in the country.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Uruguay ousts conservative government, elects leftist opposition candidate as turnout hits 90 percent
item.description: Leftist opposition candidate Yamandú Orsi claims victory in Uruguay's run-off presidential election, defeating center-right candidate Álvaro Delgado.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Georgia's newly elected parliament opens its first session with opposition boycotting
item.description: The newly elected Georgian parliament has opened its inaugural session as opposition lawmakers and the country’s president stayed away and thousands of protesters rallied outside under a heavy police presence.
item.dc:creator: Sophiko Megrelidze, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Uruguay’s leftist opposition candidate Yamandú Orsi becomes country's new president
item.description: MONTEVIDEO, Uruguay (AP) — Uruguay's leftist opposition candidate, Yamandú Orsi, became the country's new president in a tight runoff Sunday, ousting the conservative governing coalition and making the South American nation the latest to rebuke the incumbent party in a year of landmark elections wor
item.dc:creator: NAYARA BATSCHKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korea holds memorial for forced laborers at Sado mines, a day after boycotting Japanese event
item.description: SADO, Japan (AP) — South Korea paid tribute to wartime Korean forced laborers at Japan’s Sado Island Gold Mines in a memorial ceremony on Monday, a day after boycotting a similar event organized by Japan, as tensions over historical atrocities continue to strain relations between the two sides.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Landslide and flash floods hit Indonesia’s Sumatra island, leaving 16 dead and 6 missing
item.description: KARO, Indonesia (AP) — Rescuers in Indonesia recovered 16 bodies under tons of mud and rocks or that were swept away in flash floods that hit mountainside villages on Sumatra Island, officials said Monday. Six people are still missing, officials said.
item.dc:creator: BINSAR BAKKARA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: G7 foreign ministers push for Israel-Hezbollah cease-fire in final G7 of Biden administration
item.description: Foreign ministers from the world’s leading industrialized nations are expressing cautious optimism about possible progress on a cease-fire between Israel and Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Winfield, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Left-wing opposition candidate wins tight presidential election in Uruguay
item.description: MONTEVIDEO, Uruguay (AP) — Uruguay's leftist opposition candidate, Yamandú Orsi, claimed victory in a tight presidential runoff Sunday, ousting the conservative governing coalition and making the South American nation the latest to rebuke the incumbent party in a year of landmark elections.
item.dc:creator: NAYARA BATSCHKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Missing Israeli rabbi who lived in UAE found dead, 3 arrested
item.description: A missing Israeli rabbi was found dead and three suspects were arrested in connection with the incident, according to a Jewish organization, United Arab Emirates (UAE) authorities and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Far-right populist surprises in Romanian presidential election appearing set to enter runoff
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — A little-known, far-right populist took the lead in Romania’s presidential election Sunday, electoral data showed, and will likely face leftist Prime Minister Marcel Ciolacu in a runoff in two weeks, an outcome that rocked the country's political landscape.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Shootout near Israeli Embassy in Jordan leaves 3 police officers injured, gunman dead
item.description: Police in Jordan shot and killed a gunman who wounded three police officers near the Israeli Embassy on Sunday morning, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Scientists study ‘very rare’ frozen remains of 35,000-year-old saber-toothed cub
item.description: Scientists studied a three-week-old saber-toothed cub dating back 35,000 that had been left almost perfectly intact, thanks to its frozen condition.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Northern Haiti airports reopened to US flights
item.description: U.S. flights to six airports in northern Haiti can resume this week, according to the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA). The restart comes after the FAA prohibited U.S.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Catherine Herridge wants Trump 'sit-down' on bipartisan press bill
item.description: “This case involves the First Amendment and the right of reporters in the future,” Catherine Herridge said.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Death toll in Gaza from Israel-Hamas war passes 44,000, Palestinian officials say
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — The death toll in the Gaza Strip from the 13-month-old war between Israel and Hamas has surpassed 44,000, local health officials said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Netanyahu ‘in very bad company’ with ICC indictment: William Cohen
item.description: The ICC issued arrest warrants for Netanyahu and a former defense minister on war crimes allegations.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Brazil’s former President Bolsonaro and aides indicted for alleged 2022 coup attempt
item.description: Former Brazilian President Jair Bolsonaro and 36 others, including aides, were indicted by federal police on Thursday for allegedly attempting a coup to keep him in office in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
